assertions.assertion: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)

assertions.assertion: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertions.evidence: Plan lists exactly: MODIFY advisory/model/mod.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs; CREATE advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs

assertions.assertion: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertions.evidence: Commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' with 'Implements TC-9201' in footer. Follows type(scope): description format per Conventional Commits spec.

assertions.assertion: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertions.evidence: plan.md commit command includes --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code" (line 111).

assertions.assertion: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1)
The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertions.evidence: conventions.md has dedicated sections: 'Error Handling' documents Result<T, AppError> with .context() wrapping; 'Module Structure' documents model/ + service/ + endpoints/ three-layer layout. Also documents 8 additional convention categories.
